description: Concordia's sister-city before the Refusal Cascade — the place that tried to remember everything and could not refuse. After the Cascade, the city collapsed not in flame but in quiet: archives kept writing, spells kept casting, but no one remained to read them. Players arriving here find a still-functional library that still serves anyone who asks, three half-conscious archivists who are unsure if they are alive, and the Sovereign Refusal Archive itself — a monument to the moment refusal became possible. The Refusal Field is dense here; many actions are gated by glyph-strength.

anchors.name: The Sovereign Refusal Archive
anchors.description: A circular vault open to the sky; every wall is glyphs. Players arriving here can read the inscriptions of every refusal recorded since the Cascade. Combat is suspended within the anchor.

anchors.name: The Silent Library
anchors.description: Two stories of intact shelves. Books still open themselves on a schedule even though no one reads them. NPCs who enter here lose 20% combat readiness and gain 30% research yield.

anchors.name: The Collapse Memorial
anchors.description: A half-buried obelisk listing the names of every NPC who chose to stop in the Cascade rather than refuse. The list grows by one name on each anniversary of a player's first arrival.

lore_anchor_to_concordia: The Sovereign Refusal Archive surfaced first here, before any other world. The Compact between Concordia's founding factions explicitly cites the Cascade as their reason for adopting refusal-as-right.
